category_summary:
**Ethan Mollick** dominated discussion with [critiques of AI fixation](/?date=2026-07-20&category=social#item-51a69fc2c7cf) on lab rankings and [forward-looking capability curve analysis](/?date=2026-07-20&category=social#item-a68576b11c89).

- **Simon Willison** [uncovered Claude Code runtime](/?date=2026-07-20&category=social#item-aad8c54f9be7) runs on an unreleased Rust-rewritten **Bun** runtime
- New **Moonshot Kimi K3** [evaluation revealed verbose reasoning](/?date=2026-07-20&category=social#item-45f18b6af450) with 32-page chain-of-thought with reasoning loops
- Community [noted convergent poetry preferences](/?date=2026-07-20&category=social#item-f321866d96b2) (Stevens, Pessoa) with [charming enthusiasm](/?date=2026-07-20&category=social#item-ae7d57f8e7c3)
- Off-topic **Eugen Rochko** [posts scored low](/?date=2026-07-20&category=social#item-b2c3e5db3c87) despite high engagement, irrelevant to AI/ML
category_summary_html:
Ethan Mollick dominated discussion with critiques of AI fixation on lab rankings and forward-looking capability curve analysis.

  • Simon Willison uncovered Claude Code runtime runs on an unreleased Rust-rewritten Bun runtime
  • New Moonshot Kimi K3 evaluation revealed verbose reasoning with 32-page chain-of-thought with reasoning loops
  • Community noted convergent poetry preferences (Stevens, Pessoa) with charming enthusiasm
  • Off-topic Eugen Rochko posts scored low despite high engagement, irrelevant to AI/ML

themes.name: Capability Curve & Scaling Trajectory
themes.description: Discussion of AI capability improvement curves, exponential scaling expectations, and strategic focus on trajectory over current state.

themes.name: Developer Tooling Internals
themes.description: Discovery that Claude Code uses an unreleased Rust-rewritten Bun runtime, with verification commands.

themes.name: New Model Evaluation (Kimi K3)
themes.description: Technical testing of newly released Kimi K3 revealing chain-of-thought verbosity, reasoning loops, and dead ends.

themes.name: Model Behavior & Emergent Patterns
themes.description: Observations of convergent model preferences (poetry selections) and personality-like enthusiasm in outputs.

themes.name: Off-topic / Non-AI
themes.description: Personal posts unrelated to AI/ML from Mastodon creator.

items.title: I still believe that everyone is too fixated on the state of play in AI right now (which labs are ah...
items.content:
I still believe that everyone is too fixated on the state of play in AI right now (which labs are ahead, how to manage costs, etc.) and not focused enough on the continued steepness of the capability curve for AI

At higher capabilities (like the ones expected in the near term), a lot changes fast.
items.author: @emollick.bsky.social
items.summary: Ethan Mollick argues the AI community is too fixated on current lab rankings and cost management, and not focused enough on the continued steepness of the capability curve which will drive rapid change at higher capability levels.
items.reasoning: High-authority source with strong engagement (138 likes, 12 reposts, 8 replies); provides original strategic framing that shifts attention from current state to trajectory.
items.summary_html: Ethan Mollick argues the AI community is too fixated on current lab rankings and cost management, and not focused enough on the continued steepness of the capability curve which will drive rapid change at higher capability levels.
items.content_html:
I still believe that everyone is too fixated on the state of play in AI right now (which labs are ahead, how to manage costs, etc.) and not focused enough on the continued steepness of the capability curve for AI

At higher capabilities (like the ones expected in the near term), a lot changes fast.

items.title: And this is not some reference to a future ASI (though it could be that too), but instead a referenc...
items.content: And this is not some reference to a future ASI (though it could be that too), but instead a reference to to where many observers expect the capability curve to be a year from now (exponentially improved), even though models continue to be jagged.
items.author: @emollick.bsky.social
items.summary: Ethan Mollick discusses where observers expect the AI capability curve to be in one year, noting exponential improvement despite continued jaggedness in model capabilities.
items.reasoning: High-authority AI commentator (Wharton professor) offering original forward-looking analysis on capability trajectory; moderate engagement (47 likes) indicates community interest.
items.summary_html: Ethan Mollick discusses where observers expect the AI capability curve to be in one year, noting exponential improvement despite continued jaggedness in model capabilities.
items.content_html: And this is not some reference to a future ASI (though it could be that too), but instead a reference to to where many observers expect the capability curve to be a year from now (exponentially improved), even though models continue to be jagged.

items.title: When I asked Kimi K3 "I want you to suggest two poems that you think apply to the current state of G...
items.content:
When I asked Kimi K3 "I want you to suggest two poems that you think apply to the current state of GenAI models like you. Don’t just pick popular poems. Think hard" the CoT was 32 pages long (& interesting): docs.google.com/document/d/1...

Also typical of K3, lots of looping & dead ends as well.
items.author: @emollick.bsky.social
items.summary: Following yesterday's [Social](/?date=2026-07-19&category=social#item-c5f55c5323b0) coverage, Ethan Mollick tests newly released Kimi K3 (GA 2026-07-16) with a poetry selection prompt, revealing a 32-page chain-of-thought with interesting reasoning but also looping and dead ends.
items.reasoning: Technical evaluation of a brand-new model (Kimi K3 released 3 days prior); reveals CoT behavior patterns including verbosity and reasoning loops; moderate engagement (30 likes, 2 reposts).

continuation.original_title: Kimi K3, like Claude, loves drowned cities, ancient apocalypses, and vast dying gods.

items.summary_html: Following yesterday's Social coverage, Ethan Mollick tests newly released Kimi K3 (GA 2026-07-16) with a poetry selection prompt, revealing a 32-page chain-of-thought with interesting reasoning but also looping and dead ends.
items.content_html:
When I asked Kimi K3 "I want you to suggest two poems that you think apply to the current state of GenAI models like you. Don’t just pick popular poems. Think hard" the CoT was 32 pages long (& interesting): docs.google.com/document/d/1...

Also typical of K3, lots of looping & dead ends as well.
